name: Shor vs Grover Lens
summary: A comparison frame that separates qualitatively different quantum speedups: structured algebraic breakthroughs versus generic quadratic search gains.
whenToUseIt: Use it when judging application claims and deciding whether a quantum speedup would be transformational, modest, or irrelevant in practice.
steps: Ask whether the problem has exploitable mathematical structure.
Check whether the claimed speedup looks exponential, polynomial, or only quadratic.
Estimate the data-loading and oracle assumptions.
Compare the quantum resource cost to realistic classical baselines.
Decide whether the application claim survives engineering reality.
example: Factoring and unstructured search are often both cited in quantum discussions, but they represent very different levels of practical disruption and hardware demand.
